surface.name: Splunk Search Tutorial
surface.selection_basis: Splunk's official documentation home spans Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform, Enterprise Security, SOAR, ITSI, Observability Cloud, AppDynamics, and developer tooling. The Search Tutorial is the general guided path for adding and searching data in the Splunk platform, but it presents Splunk Cloud Platform and Splunk Enterprise as peer trial choices rather than selecting one company-wide default.

alternatives_considered.name: Splunk Cloud Platform trial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Search Tutorial offers this hosted route alongside the self-managed Splunk Enterprise trial and does not establish it as the universal default.

alternatives_considered.name: Splunk Enterprise trial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route adds an operating-system-specific installer and local installation before Splunk Web, while the tutorial treats it as a peer of Splunk Cloud Platform.

alternatives_considered.name: Splunk Observability Cloud
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The official documentation navigation presents Observability Cloud as a separate product surface with its own ingestion and verification journeys.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Splunk account, choose a trial platform, upload the official tutorial data, and confirm that Splunk indexed it as events.
assumed_prior_state: No Splunk.com account, Splunk Cloud Platform instance, Splunk Enterprise installation, tutorial data, or indexed event is assumed.
A supported browser and a computer capable of downloading the tutorial ZIP files are available.
No signup, trial creation, terms acceptance, download, installation, upload, or search was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Success! The results confirm that the data in the tutorialdata.zip file was indexed and that events were created.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is the Search app showing automatically returned events from the uploaded tutorial data, but the no-account route cannot be reconstructed through the current post-submit account transition.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Search app displays tutorial-data events after Start Searching; this state is downstream of the undocumented account-creation result.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The upload guide explicitly labels indexed tutorial events as Success, but the required account transition is not publicly established end to end.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Search Tutorial explicitly marks indexed events as success before teaching searches, reports, charts, and dashboards. The account form and tutorial do not state the observable result, verification sequence, or convergence after Create Your Account, so the complete first-user path stops before that terminal.

prerequisites.requirement: A Splunk.com account is required to access the free trial software.
The public account form requires agreement to the Splunk Website Terms and Conditions of Use, Splunk Privacy Policy, and Splunk General Terms.
The developer must choose Splunk Cloud Platform or Splunk Enterprise for the tutorial.
The tutorial requires the compressed `tutorialdata.zip` and later `Prices.csv.zip`; `tutorialdata.zip` must remain compressed.
Splunk Cloud Platform requires a current supported Chrome, Firefox, or Safari browser; Splunk Enterprise additionally requires a supported operating system and the documented minimum hardware.

primary_path.action: Open the Search Tutorial prerequisites and review the required account, trial-platform choice, system requirements, and tutorial files.
primary_path.output: The official Cloud Platform and Enterprise trial branches and indexed-event terminal are identified.
primary_path.success_signal: The page shows Create a splunk.com account, Choose a platform, Download the tutorial data files, and Access the Trial version sections.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No recovery is documented for choosing the wrong product surface.

primary_path.action: Open the Splunk account form and enter the required registration details.
details: The public form exposes Business Email, Password, First Name, Last Name, Job Title, Phone Number, Company, Zip or Postal Code, a required legal agreement, and an optional marketing agreement.
primary_path.input: Business and identity details plus acceptance of the required terms
primary_path.output: The Create Your Account control becomes the documented submission action.
primary_path.success_signal: The required public fields and button are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No public source used here documents validation, identity verification, email confirmation, or recovery behavior.

primary_path.action: Stop before selecting Create Your Account because the public sources do not establish the post-submit account state.
primary_path.input: Completed but unsubmitted public account form
primary_path.output: No account submission or external state change occurs.
primary_path.success_signal: The last independently observable public boundary is the unsubmitted Create Your Account control.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The verification, provisioning, or authenticated-dashboard transition after submission is not publicly documented.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The public form and tutorial do not document the result of Create Your Account. After that hidden transition, a Splunk Cloud Platform candidate would choose the Cloud trial, download both tutorial ZIP files without uncompressing them, confirm the robot check, select Start Trial, wait for the Your Splunk Cloud Platform Trial is Ready confirmation, select View My Instance, accept the Terms of Service, use the instance URL and `sc_admin` credentials from email if needed, close the welcome uploader, open Settings then Add Data, choose Upload, select `tutorialdata.zip`, continue to Input Settings, choose Segment in path with segment 1, select Review, Submit, and Start Searching, then observe indexed tutorial events.
candidate_path_gap.impact: Neither the Cloud candidate nor the Enterprise candidate can be connected to a new visitor without inventing account completion, and the official tutorial also leaves the platform choice to the developer.

branches.condition: The developer chooses Splunk Cloud Platform
branches.path: Start the hosted trial, accept its Terms of Service, open the provisioned instance, and use the Add Data wizard.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids a local installer but adds trial provisioning, instance credentials, and Cloud terms acceptance.

branches.path: Choose the operating-system-specific installer, download and install Splunk Enterprise, launch Splunk Web, and then use the same Add Data wizard.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds operating-system, hardware, installer, local-admin, and installation actions before upload.

friction_gates.description: The observable state after public account-form submission is not documented.
The Search Tutorial requires a choice between hosted Splunk Cloud Platform and self-managed Splunk Enterprise.
The Cloud candidate requires Splunk Cloud Platform Terms of Service acceptance before the instance opens.
The tutorial requires two ZIP downloads and warns that `tutorialdata.zip` must remain compressed.

sources.title: What you need for this tutorial | Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform
sections_used: Access the Trial version of the Splunk software
evidence_supported: Cloud trial provisioning and terms sequence

sources.title: Get a Splunk.com Account | Splunk
evidence_supported: absence of a documented post-submit state

sources.title: Splunk Cloud Platform Free Trial | Splunk
Upload the tutorial data | Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform

uncertainties.question: What exact verification and authenticated transition follows Create Your Account?
uncertainties.impact: The journey cannot connect a new visitor to the trial selector or instance without inventing required account states.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official form exposes inputs and a submit control, while the tutorial only says to complete registration information and then assumes an account.

uncertainties.question: Which Splunk product and deployment model should represent the company's general first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Cloud and Enterprise have different provisioning, credential, installation, and system-requirement paths, while other Splunk product families have separate journeys.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation navigation and Search Tutorial do not select a universal default.

uncertainties.question: Is the current Splunk Cloud Platform trial 14 or 15 days?
uncertainties.impact: The plan-duration claim is contradictory, although this does not change the indexed-event terminal.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current trial landing page says 14 days while the Search Tutorial page says 15 days.
